::: {.chapter-num}
Chapter II
:::

## Through time {.chapter-title}

::: {.chapter-lede}
A year of mornings becomes a year of measurements.
Three numbers carry most of the weight in Visible — Stability Score, Heart Rate Variability (HRV), and Resting Heart Rate — and looking at how they've moved is usually where insight starts.
:::

::: {.analysis-section}

### Smoothed trends

Each dot is one day.
The curved line through the dots is a **trend line** — it ignores the noise of any one morning and shows the underlying direction.
Look for the slope of that line: a slow climb in HRV or Stability over months is a real signal even if any individual day looks all over the place.
A drift downward in HRV alongside a climb in Resting Heart Rate is a classic pattern when the body is under more strain than usual.
The two **shaded bands** are the *most stable* and *hardest* stretches called out at the top of the page — drawn here so you can see where they sit on the underlying timeline of all three measures.

```{webr}
#| label: chart-trends
#| input: ["fileText", "darkMode"]

if (exists("visible")) {
  viz <- viz_theme(darkMode)

  stretch_layers <- list()
  if (!is.null(best_stretch)) {
    stretch_layers <- c(stretch_layers, list(
      annotate("rect",
               xmin = best_stretch$start, xmax = best_stretch$end,
               ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf,
               fill = viz$secondary, alpha = 0.18)
    ))
  }
  if (!is.null(worst_stretch)) {
    stretch_layers <- c(stretch_layers, list(
      annotate("rect",
               xmin = worst_stretch$start, xmax = worst_stretch$end,
               ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf,
               fill = viz$accent, alpha = 0.18)
    ))
  }

  caption_txt <- paste0(
    "Each dot is a day · the line is a smoothed trend",
    if (is_multi_year) " · dotted lines mark new years" else "",
    if (length(stretch_layers) > 0)
      " · green band = most stable stretch · pink band = hardest"
    else ""
  )

  visible |>
    filter(tracker %in% c("HR Variability", "Resting HR",
                                 "Stability Score")) |>
    ggplot(aes(odate, value)) +
    stretch_layers +
    year_marks(visible$odate, viz, is_multi_year) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.3, colour = viz$secondary) +
    geom_smooth(method = "loess", formula = y ~ x, se = TRUE,
                colour = viz$accent, fill = viz$accent_soft) +
    facet_wrap(~ tracker, scales = "free_y", ncol = 1) +
    labs(x = NULL, y = NULL,
         title = "Core measurements over time",
         subtitle = "Stability, HR Variability and Resting HR across your full timeline",
         caption = caption_txt) +
    viz$theme(base_size = 13)
}
```

:::

::: {.analysis-section}

### Stability calendar

```{webr}
#| label: fact-calendar
#| input: ["fileText"]

if (exists("visible")) {
  stab <- visible[visible$tracker == "Stability Score" &
                    !is.na(visible$value), ]
  if (nrow(stab) >= 7) {
    d_range <- range(visible$odate, na.rm = TRUE)
    span <- as.integer(d_range[2] - d_range[1]) + 1L
    logged <- length(unique(stab$odate))
    pct <- round(100 * logged / span)
    median_stab <- stats::median(stab$value, na.rm = TRUE)

    htmltools::HTML(sprintf(
      paste0(
        '<p class="section-fact">',
        'Stability logged on <strong>%s of %s days</strong> ',
        '(<strong>%s%%</strong> complete). ',
        'Your typical day sits at <strong>%.1f / 5</strong> (median). ',
        'Median is used instead of mean because a handful of unusually ',
        'good or rough days can drag an average around — the median is ',
        'the value half your days fall above and half below, so it stays ',
        'closer to your everyday baseline.',
        '</p>'
      ),
      format(logged, big.mark = ","),
      format(span, big.mark = ","),
      pct, median_stab
    ))
  }
}
```

Each tile is a day.
Colour shows the morning Stability Score (1–5).
Reads like a wall calendar: weeks march left-to-right, weekdays stack top-to-bottom.
Muted tiles are days you didn't log.
Look for streaks (long runs of similar colour) and breaks (a sudden colour change) — those are the moments worth pairing with whatever was happening in your life that week.

```{webr}
#| label: chart-calendar
#| input: ["fileText", "darkMode"]
#| fig-width: 12
#| fig-height: 6

if (exists("visible")) {
  viz <- viz_theme(darkMode)

  date_range <- range(visible$odate, na.rm = TRUE)
  all_days <- data.frame(
    odate = seq.Date(date_range[1], date_range[2], by = "day")
  )
  all_days$week_start <- floor_date(all_days$odate, "week",
                                                week_start = 1)
  all_days$wday <- wday(all_days$odate, label = TRUE,
                                    abbr = TRUE, week_start = 1)

  stab <- visible |>
    filter(tracker == "Stability Score") |>
    group_by(odate) |>
    summarise(value = mean(value, na.rm = TRUE),
                     .groups = "drop")

  grid <- merge(all_days, stab, by = "odate", all.x = TRUE)

  median_stab <- stats::median(stab$value, na.rm = TRUE)

  base <- ggplot(mapping = aes(fill = value)) +
    scale_y_discrete(limits = rev, expand = c(0, 0)) +
    scale_fill_gradient(low = viz$cal_low, high = viz$cal_high,
                        limits = c(1, 5), na.value = viz$cal_na,
                        name = "Stability") +
    coord_cartesian(clip = "off") +
    labs(x = NULL, y = NULL,
         title = "Stability Score across your timeline",
         subtitle = if (is_multi_year)
           "One row per year · each tile is one day"
         else
           "Each tile is one day",
         caption = sprintf(
           "Brighter = more stable · muted tile = no entry · median %.1f / 5",
           median_stab)) +
    viz$theme(base_size = 11) +
    theme(
      panel.grid = element_blank(),
      axis.text.x = element_text(hjust = 0),
      axis.ticks = element_blank(),
      legend.position = "right",
      legend.key.height = grid::unit(1.2, "cm")
    )

  if (is_multi_year) {
    grid$year <- year(grid$odate)
    grid$woy <- as.integer(format(grid$odate, "%V"))
    grid$year_iso <- as.integer(format(grid$odate, "%G"))
    grid$year <- ifelse(grid$woy >= 52 & month(grid$odate) == 1,
                        year(grid$odate) - 1,
                        ifelse(grid$woy == 1 & month(grid$odate) == 12,
                               year(grid$odate) + 1,
                               year(grid$odate)))
    month_weeks <- as.integer(format(
      as.Date(sprintf("2024-%02d-15", 1:12)), "%V"))
    base +
      geom_tile(data = grid,
                aes(x = woy, y = wday),
                colour = viz$paper_card, linewidth = 0.5) +
      facet_wrap(~ year, ncol = 1, strip.position = "left") +
      scale_x_continuous(breaks = month_weeks, labels = month.abb,
                         expand = c(0.005, 0.005)) +
      theme(strip.text.y.left = element_text(angle = 0, face = "italic",
                                              colour = viz$ink_soft,
                                              size = 11))
  } else {
    base +
      geom_tile(data = grid,
                aes(x = week_start, y = wday),
                colour = viz$paper_card, linewidth = 0.5) +
      scale_x_date(date_breaks = "1 month", date_labels = "%b %Y",
                   expand = c(0.005, 0.005))
  }
}
```

:::
